# Risk Envelope Schema

The risk envelope is a machine-readable YAML file at
`<workspace>/risk-envelope.yaml`. It is the contract every proposal
must satisfy. Created by `bootstrap.yaml`. Edited only by the
operator (or by `weekly-review.yaml` proposing changes that require
operator approval via fermata).

## Schema

```yaml
# risk-envelope.yaml — generated by bootstrap, edited by operator
version: 1

caps:
  per_position_pct: 5.0          # max % portfolio in one ticker
  per_sector_pct: 25.0           # max % in one sector
  per_thesis_family_count: 2     # max simultaneous positions per family
  max_open_positions: 12
  cash_floor_pct: 10.0           # min cash %
  daily_loss_cap_pct: 2.0        # halt entries if single-day loss exceeds
  weekly_drawdown_halt_pct: 8.0  # weekly review writes fermata if exceeded
  per_position_initial_stop_pct: 7.0   # default initial stop (technical may override deeper)
  trailing_stop_min_pct: 10.0    # midday tightening floor

allowed:
  asset_classes: [equity, etf]
  order_types: [market, limit, stop_limit]

forbidden:
  asset_classes: [option, future, crypto]
  actions: [short_sell, margin_buy, inverse_etf_unhedged]

thesis_families:
  - ai_infrastructure
  - energy_transition
  - biotech
  - consumer_staples
  - financials
  - cyclicals
  - defensives
  - other

operator_overrides:
  # operator-only: hand-edited symbols to never trade
  blocklist: []
  # operator-only: positions to keep even if envelope would close them
  allowlist_holds: []
```

## Enforcement

The deterministic check at `_scripts/check_envelope.sh <proposals.json>`
parses a proposal slate and exits non-zero if any proposal violates
the envelope. The market-open phase calls this twice — once after
pre-market hands off, and once again after red/blue arbitration
narrows the slate.

The check is intentionally pessimistic. Edge cases default to fail.
A proposal with a missing `thesis_family` field fails the check (no
implicit defaults — the agent must classify).

## Relationship to operator authority

The envelope is sovereign over all phases. Phases cannot bypass it.
If the agent thinks a HIGH-conviction proposal "deserves" to exceed
caps, the proposal goes to `proposals/PENDING-COMPOSER-REVIEW.md`
with the agent's argument. The operator decides whether to widen the
envelope or reject the proposal.

This separation — envelope enforcement deterministic, envelope
adjustment operator-only — is the architectural commitment that
makes the family safe to run unattended.
